text.title: Rails 7+ Backend Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the Rails 7+ methodology end-to-end.

step.name: lock-serviceresult
step.action: Create `app/services/service_result.rb` with `success?`, `failure?`, `value`, `errors`. Add CI grep that fails on additional `class ServiceResult` definitions.
step.decision-gate: If a second ServiceResult appears, BLOCK per `one-serviceresult-class` rule.

step.name: extract-service-per-verb
step.action: For each verb create `<Domain>::<Verb>Service` with `initialize(params:, current_user:)` and `call` returning `ServiceResult`. Controllers do Strong Params then `Service.new(params: permitted, current_user: current_user).call`.
step.decision-gate: If service touches `params` or HTTP globals, BLOCK per `strong-params-not-into-service` rule.

step.name: transaction-and-after-commit
step.action: Wrap writes in `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do ... end`. Move mailers / webhooks / search indexing to `after_create_commit` callbacks or chained Sidekiq jobs after the transaction returns.
step.decision-gate: If `deliver_later` lands inside the transaction, BLOCK per `after-commit-for-side-effects` rule.

step.name: sidekiq-with-ids-and-retry
step.action: Declare `perform(record_id, ...)` with primitives. Add `sidekiq_options retry: 5, dead: true` + `sidekiq_retry_in` per exception. Inside `perform` reload the record from DB.
step.decision-gate: If AR object passed as arg, BLOCK per `sidekiq-jobs-take-ids` rule.

step.name: rspec-per-branch
step.action: Add request specs (`spec/requests/...`) for controller paths and service specs (`spec/services/...`) for branches. Use FactoryBot `build_stubbed` where persistence isn't needed.
